heading: Preservation of enforcement powers of States
label: 5552 Preservation of enforcement powers of States

children.heading: Action by State
children.label: 1 Action by State
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), the attorney general (or the equivalent thereof) of any State may bring a civil action in the name of such State in any district court of the United States in that State or in State court that is located in that State and that has jurisdiction over the defendant, to enforce provisions of this title or regulations issued under this title, and to secure remedies under provisions of this title or remedies otherwise provided under other law. A State regulator may bring a civil action or other appropriate proceeding to enforce the provisions of this title or regulations issued under this title with respect to any entity that is State-chartered, incorporated, licensed, or otherwise authorized to do business under State law (except as provided in paragraph (2)), and to secure remedies under provisions of this title or remedies otherwise provided under other provisions of law with respect to such an entity.111111See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Action by State against national bank or Federal savings association to enforce rules
children.label: 2 Action by State against national bank or Federal savings association to enforce rules

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as permitted under subparagraph (B), the attorney general (or equivalent thereof) of any State may not bring a civil action in the name of such State against a national bank or Federal savings association to enforce a provision of this title.1

children.heading: Enforcement of rules permitted
children.label: B Enforcement of rules permitted
children.text: The attorney general (or the equivalent thereof) of any State may bring a civil action in the name of such State against a national bank or Federal savings association in any district court of the United States in the State or in State court that is located in that State and that has jurisdiction over the defendant to enforce a regulation prescribed by the Bureau under a provision of this title and to secure remedies under provisions of this title or remedies otherwise provided under other law.11

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 3 Rule of construction
children.text: No provision of this title shall be construed as modifying, limiting, or superseding the operation of any provision of an enumerated consumer law that relates to the authority of a State attorney general or State regulator to enforce such Federal law.1

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Before initiating any action in a court or other administrative or regulatory proceeding against any covered person as authorized by subsection (a) to enforce any provision of this title, including any regulation prescribed by the Bureau under this title, a State attorney general or State regulator shall timely provide a copy of the complete complaint to be filed and written notice describing such action or proceeding to the Bureau and the prudential regulator, if any, or the designee thereof.11

children.heading: Emergency action
children.label: B Emergency action
children.text: If prior notice is not practicable, the State attorney general or State regulator shall provide a copy of the complete complaint and the notice to the Bureau and the prudential regulator, if any, immediately upon instituting the action or proceeding.

children.label: ii
children.text: the alleged facts underlying the proceeding; and

children.label: iii
children.text: whether there may be a need to coordinate the prosecution of the proceeding so as not to interfere with any action, including any rulemaking, undertaken by the Bureau, a prudential regulator, or another Federal agency.

children.label: A
children.text: intervene in the action as a party;

children.label: i
children.text: remove the action to the appropriate United States district court, if the action was not originally brought there; and

children.label: ii
children.text: be heard on all matters arising in the action; and

children.label: C
children.text: appeal any order or judgment, to the same extent as any other party in the proceeding may.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: c Regulations
children.text: The Bureau shall prescribe regulations to implement the requirements of this section and, from time to time, provide guidance in order to further coordinate actions with the State attorneys general and other regulators.

children.heading: State claims
children.label: 1 State claims
children.text: No provision of this section shall be construed as altering, limiting, or affecting the authority of a State attorney general or any other regulatory or enforcement agency or authority to bring an action or other regulatory proceeding arising solely under the law in effect in that State.

children.heading: State securities regulators
children.label: 2 State securities regulators
children.text: No provision of this title shall be construed as altering, limiting, or affecting the authority of a State securities commission (or any agency or office performing like functions) under State law to adopt rules, initiate enforcement proceedings, or take any other action with respect to a person regulated by such commission or authority.1

children.heading: State insurance regulators
children.label: 3 State insurance regulators
children.text: No provision of this title shall be construed as altering, limiting, or affecting the authority of a State insurance commission or State insurance regulator under State law to adopt rules, initiate enforcement proceedings, or take any other action with respect to a person regulated by such commission or regulator.1
